title: Not Equal To Operator (!=) in Java

content.problemStatement:
In Java, the not equal to (`!=`) operator is used to compare two primitive values. It checks whether the values on both sides of the operator are different and returns a boolean result (`true` or `false`).

---

**Comparing Integers**
The `!=` operator can be used to compare integer values:
```java
int num1 = 10; // num1 holds an integer value of 10
int num2 = 20; // num2 holds an integer value of 20
System.out.println(num1 != num2);  // Outputs: true
```

**Comparing Floating-Point Numbers**
The `!=` operator also works with floating-point numbers (`double` and `float`):
```java
double price1 = 5.99; // price1 holds a double value of 5.99
double price2 = 5.99; // price2 holds a double value of 5.99
System.out.println(price1 != price2);  // Outputs: false
```

**Comparing Float and Int**
```java
float a = 10.0f; // a holds the float value 10.0
int b = 10; // b holds the integer value 10
System.out.println(a != b);  // Outputs: false
```
